rationale.R1.1: The artifact makes an explicit, named contribution claim ('fully decentralized verification pipeline') that is testable against the described implementation; assessing whether the stated thesis holds is central to any substantive review of this excerpt.
rationale.R1.2: Load-bearing: the paper claims 'no cross-shard coordination' and 'fully decentralized' operation in paragraphs 1–2, then in paragraph 3 introduces synchronous Paxos-based replication across all shards for threshold recalibration every 30 seconds. This is a direct internal contradiction that invalidates the core architectural claim. Additionally, the graceful-degradation-under-partition argument in paragraph 4 is undermined by the Paxos dependency (Paxos requires a quorum and cannot make progress under certain partitions). Logical consistency is the load-bearing axis here.
rationale.R1.3: The excerpt cites concrete empirical numbers (4.2M submissions/sec, 12x throughput, equivalent F1, MTurk-LLM benchmark match) without providing enough experimental context to evaluate them; verifying that these claims are adequately grounded is a central responsibility given the quantitative specificity.

parsed.clarification_question: Should the review focus on technical and logical critique (internal consistency of architecture claims, evidence support for performance numbers) — or primarily on writing quality and prose polish?
